pi_items.source: Verified against Bridge Command scenario structure documentation. Path format is correct for Bridge Command installation.
Verified through geographical reference sources. 50.80N is within the English Channel area, specifically in the eastern Channel region.
Verified through geographical reference sources. 0.50E is within the English Channel area. Combined with center_lat, this places the scenario center in the eastern English Channel.
Verified through manual bearing calculation. A course of 045 degrees (northeast) is consistent with the description and the merchant/escort positioning logic.
Stated in task description and used consistently throughout all vessel definitions in the task setup.
Stated in task description as the longitude at which all vessels execute a second waypoint leg turning to 090 degrees.
Stated in task description as the course all 6 vessels turn to at waypoint 2 (longitude 1.00E).
Verified through manual bearing/distance calculations. Verifier.py confirms escort vessels (HMS Guardian, HMS Defender) are positioned 3nm from convoy center.
Verified through manual bearing/distance calculations. Merchant column ships are spaced 1nm apart (Lead 1nm ahead at 045°, Guide at center, Rear 1nm astern at 225°).

Metadata claims 0.3nm tolerance, but verifier.py line 91 uses: dist_ok = abs(dist - 3.0) < 0.5 for ownship, and line 121 uses abs(dist - 1.0) < 0.3 for some merchants. For ownship, actual tolerance is 0.5nm, contradicting metadata.
Metadata claims 5.0° tolerance, but verifier.py uses larger tolerances: line 92 has brg_ok = abs(brg - 315.0) < 10.0 for ownship, and similar 10° tolerances for otherships (lines 121, 126, 131, 136).
Metadata claims 1.0 knot tolerance but verifier.py does not check vessel speeds, making this claim unverifiable from code analysis.

pi_items.verified_value: Patrol vessel / Fast Inshore Patrol Vessel (FIPV) or Patrol Craft (historical ship name)
pi_items.source: Web search found HMS Sentinel (P246) is a current FIPV/Patrol vessel, not a Frigate. Historical HMS Sentinel ships (1904 scout cruiser, 1945 submarine, 1975 patrol vessel) exist but no current frigate named HMS Sentinel in Royal Navy.

pi_items.verified_value: Historical ship name only - no current HMS Guardian in active service
pi_items.source: Web search found HMS Guardian has historical use only: HMS Guardian (1784) - 44-gun ship, HMS Guardian (1932) - net laying ship, HMS Guardian (Falklands) - support vessel (1980s). No current active Royal Navy ship named HMS Guardian.

pi_items.verified_value: HMS Defender (D36) is a current Daring-class destroyer
pi_items.source: Verified via Royal Navy official website and ship tracking sources. HMS Defender (D36) is a Type 45 Daring-class destroyer, commissioned 2013, currently undergoing refit (due back in service Summer 2026).

Parameter not found in official Bridge Command configuration documentation (https://www.bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/config.php). Standard Bridge Command parameters include arpa_on, full_radar, radar_mode but not max_radar_range.
Parameter not found in official Bridge Command configuration documentation. Bridge Command uses radar_sensitivity and radar_range_sensitivity, not radar_range_resolution.
Verified via Bridge Command official documentation. Parameter arpa_on: if set to 1, radar starts with ARPA active.
Verified via Bridge Command official documentation. Parameter full_radar: if set to 1, radar starts maximized.
Verified through manual bearing calculation using planar approximation (1° lat = 60nm, 1° lon = 60*cos(lat) nm). Bearing 315° (NW) is correctly calculated.
Verified through manual distance calculation using Pythagorean theorem on planar coordinates. Distance correctly calculated as 3.0nm.

Setting name not verified in Bridge Command documentation. Coordinates (50.80N, 0.50E) are in eastern English Channel region, but exact scenario setting name 'English Channel East' could not be verified.
Specific date and time cannot be verified without access to archived Bridge Command files or task execution history. Historical weather data for this date not accessible.

privileged_info_summary: The convoy escort scenario is synthetically generated using the Bridge Command navigation simulator. Core geographical and mathematical parameters are verified: the English Channel center position (50.80N, 0.50E) is accurate; bearing and distance calculations are mathematically correct; the formation geometry is properly specified with ownship at 3nm at 315°, merchant column at 1nm spacing, and escort screen at 3nm radius. However, critical discrepancies exist: the metadata specifies tolerances (0.3nm distance, 5.0° bearing) that contradict the verifier logic which uses 0.5nm and 10.0° tolerances. Ship designations are problematic: HMS Sentinel is not a current frigate (it's a patrol vessel), HMS Guardian does not exist in the current Royal Navy (historical name only), though HMS Defender (D36) is a real active Daring-class destroyer. Configuration parameters max_radar_range (72) and radar_range_resolution (256) do not appear in official Bridge Command documentation, suggesting they may be custom additions without standard support. The ARPA radar configuration settings (arpa_on=1, full_radar=1) are valid Bridge Command parameters.
